The most recent stable release of the CPython language at this time is **Python 3.14.3** (January 2026).  

| Release | Date | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Python 3.14.3** | Feb 3 2026 | Latest patch release of the 3.14 series, includes security fixes, bug‑fixes, and minor feature improvements. |
| **Python 3.13.12** | Feb 3 2026 | Current patch release of the 3.13 series (support ends in 2028). |
| **Python 3.12.13** | Mar 3 2026 | Current patch release of the 3.12 series (support ends in 2031). |
| **Python 3.11.15** | Mar 3 2026 | Current patch release of the 3.11 series (long‑term support until 2032). |
| **Python 3.10.20** | Mar 3 2026 | Current patch release of the 3.10 series (end of life soon). |

You can download the binaries and source code for Python 3.14.3 from the official Python downloads page:  
👉 <https://www.python.org/downloads/>  

If you’re looking to use the newest features and improvements, 3.14.3 is the recommended version. If you need a longer‑term support branch, 3.13 or 3.12 are still available.
What are the key features of this version?
